rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing features or declared variables.
Generate syntactically valid AGREE contracts within annex agree blocks using proper scope placement.
generic_rules: Ensure all AGREE expressions reference visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Validate that assumption and guarantee types match the actual data types of referenced features.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Use boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type contracts should contain assumptions and guarantees for ports and features.
Implementation contracts should only include assignments for output features or auxiliary variables.
Boolean range checks should use exact boolean expressions when the port is of type bool.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL property names directly as AGREE variables.
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons.
Do not generate clauses without visible identifiers or concrete bounds.
